title: देवी कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री देवी कवचम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्रीदेवीकवचस्य ब्रह्मा ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीमहालक्ष्मीर्देवता।
श्रीदेवीप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

ॐ नमश्चण्डिकायै॥

ब्रह्मोवाच -

ॐ ऐं ह्रीं क्लीं चामुण्डायै विच्चे।
नमो देव्यै महादेव्यै शिवायै सततं नमः।
नमः प्रकृत्यै भद्रायै नियताः प्रणताः स्म ताम्॥१॥

रौद्रायै नमो नित्यायै गौर्यै धात्र्यै नमो नमः।
ज्योत्स्नायै चेन्दुरूपिण्यै सुखायै सततं नमः॥२॥

कल्याण्यै प्रणतां वृद्ध्यै सिद्ध्यै कूर्मो नमो नमः।
नैर्ऋत्यै भूभृतां लक्ष्म्यै शर्वाण्यै ते नमो नमः॥३॥

दुर्गायै दुर्गपारायै सारायै सर्वकारिण्यै।
ख्यात्यै तथैव कृष्णायै धूम्रायै सततं नमः॥४॥

अतिसौम्यातिरौद्रायै नतास्तस्यै नमो नमः।
नमो जगत्प्रतिष्ठायै देव्यै कृत्यै नमो नमः॥५॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु विष्णुमायेति शब्दिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥६॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु चेतनेत्यभिधीयते।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥७॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु बुद्धिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥८॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु निद्रारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥९॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु क्षुधारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१०॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु छायारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥११॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु शक्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१२॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु तृष्णारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१३॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु क्षान्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१४॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु जातिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१५॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु लज्जारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१६॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु शान्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१७॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु श्रद्धारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१८॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु कान्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१९॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु लक्ष्मीरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥२०॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु वृत्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥२१॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु स्मृतिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥२२॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु दयारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥२३॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु तुष्टिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥२४॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु मातृरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥२५॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु भ्रान्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥२६॥

इन्द्रियाणामधिष्ठात्री भूतानां चाखिलेषु या।
भूतेषु सततं तस्यै व्याप्तिदेव्यै नमो नमः॥२७॥

चितिरूपेण या कृत्स्नमेतद्व्याप्य स्थिता जगत्।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥२८॥

॥ इति श्री देवी कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Devi Kavacham ||

Om Asya Shri Devi-kavachasya Brahma Rishiph.
Anushtup Chhandah. Shri Mahalakshmir Devata.
Shri Devi-prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

Om Namash-chandikayai.

Brahma Uvacha -

Om Aim Hrim Klim Chamundayai Vichche.
Namo Devyai Mahadevyai Shivayai Satatam Namah.
Namah Prakrityai Bhadrayai Niyatah Pranatah Sma Tam.||1||

Raudrayai Namo Nityayai Gauryai Dhatryai Namo Namah.
Jyotsnayai Chendru-rupinyai Sukhayai Satatam Namah.||2||

Kalyanyai Pranatam Vriddhyai Siddhyai Kurmo Namo Namah.
Nairityai Bhubhritam Lakshmyai Sharvanyai Te Namo Namah.||3||

Durgayai Durga-parayai Sarayai Sarva-karinyai.
Khyatyai Tathaiva Krishnayai Dhumrayai Satatam Namah.||4||

Ati-saumya-ati-raudrayai Natas-tasyai Namo Namah.
Namo Jagat-pratishthayai Devyai Krityai Namo Namah.||5||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Vishnu-mayeti Shabdita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||6||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Chetanety-abhidhiyate.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||7||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Buddhi-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||8||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Nidra-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||9||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Kshudha-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||10||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Chhaya-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||11||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Shakti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||12||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Trishna-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||13||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Kshanti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||14||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Jati-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||15||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Lajja-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||16||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Shanti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||17||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Shraddha-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||18||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Kanti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||19||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Lakshmi-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||20||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Vritti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||21||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Smriti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||22||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Daya-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||23||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Tushti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||24||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Matri-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||25||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Bhranti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||26||

Indriyanam Adhishthhatri Bhutanam Chakhileshu Ya.
Bhuteshu Satatam Tasyai Vyapti-devyai Namo Namah.||27||

Chiti-rupena Ya Kritsnam Etad Vyapya Sthita Jagat.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||28||

|| Iti Shri Devi Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ब्रह्मा बोले - देवी को, महादेवी को, शिवा को सदा नमस्कार है। प्रकृति को, भद्रा को नमस्कार है।

रौद्रा को, नित्या को, गौरी को, धात्री को नमस्कार है। ज्योत्स्ना को, चन्द्ररूपिणी को, सुखस्वरूपा को सदा नमस्कार है।

जो देवी सब प्राणियों में विष्णुमाया कहलाती हैं, उन्हें नमस्कार, नमस्कार, नमस्कार।

जो देवी सब प्राणियों में चेतना कहलाती हैं, बुद्धि रूप में स्थित हैं, निद्रा रूप में, क्षुधा रूप में, छाया रूप में, शक्ति रूप में, तृष्णा रूप में, क्षान्ति रूप में, लज्जा रूप में, शान्ति रूप में, श्रद्धा रूप में, कान्ति रूप में, लक्ष्मी रूप में, स्मृति रूप में, दया रूप में, तुष्टि रूप में, मातृ रूप में स्थित हैं - उन्हें बारम्बार नमस्कार।

जो चिति रूप से इस सम्पूर्ण जगत् को व्याप्त करके स्थित हैं, उन देवी को नमस्कार, नमस्कार, नमस्कार।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीदेवीकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य ब्रह्मा ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीमहालक्ष्मीर्देवता। ऐं ह्रीं क्लीं बीजम्। चामुण्डा शक्तिः। श्रीदेवीप्रीत्यर्थे देवीकवचजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Provides complete divine armor of protection from the Goddess
Invokes all forms and manifestations of the Divine Mother
Invokes wisdom, peace, prosperity, and spiritual illumination
Essential part of Devi Mahatmyam recitation
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears and bestows courage and inner strength
Invokes the Goddess in her 28 cosmic forms for total protection
description: The Devi Kavacham, known in Sanskrit as देवी कवचम्, is a divine armor prayer for protection in praise of Goddess Durga. Drawn from Devi Mahatmyam (Markandeya Purana), this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to invoke all forms and manifestations of the Divine Mother, remove all fears and bestow courage and inner strength, and provide complete divine armor of protection from the Goddess. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri. The text contains 28 verses and takes around 12 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Devi Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Devi Kavacham from Devi Mahatmyam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred armor hymn invoking all forms of the Goddess for divine protection.
relatedSearches: Devi Kavacham 47 shlokas Sanskrit Devanagari lyrics
Devi Kavacham English translation with meaning
Devi Kavacham nine forms Navadurga body parts
Devi Kavacham anga of Saptashati Markandeya Purana
Devi Kavacham benefits of daily chanting
Devi Kavacham before Argala and Keelaka order
Devi Kavach phalashruti protection from all directions
Devi Kavacham audio with svara recitation

seoFaqs.question: Where exactly does Devi Kavacham fit within the Durga Saptashati patha-vidhi?
seoFaqs.answer: Devi Kavacham is the first of the three preliminary angas (limbs) recited before the thirteen chapters of the Devi Mahatmya, followed by Argala Stotram and Keelaka Stotram. Traditional Saptashati patha-vidhi prescribes the order Kavacham, Argala, Keelaka, then Ratri Suktam, the thirteen adhyayas of the Mahatmya, Devi Suktam and finally Vedokta Rahasya Trayam. The Kavacham is recited first because it is considered the protective armour that must envelop the reciter before the more powerful Mahatmya verses are taken up. Skipping the Kavacham is held by orthodox tradition to leave the reciter exposed to the intense shakti released by the main text. (Sources: devimahatmya.com; mahaperiyavaa.blog; Drikpanchang Saptashati Patha Vidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Why do some published versions show 47 verses while others show 55 or 61?
seoFaqs.answer: The verse count discrepancy comes from regional manuscript traditions and the inclusion or exclusion of phalashruti and dhyana verses. The core Markandeya-samvada body of the Kavacham, in which Brahma describes the nine Durgas and their protection of specific body parts, is consistently 47 shlokas in most South Indian editions. Northern and Bengali editions often include an additional viniyoga, dhyana sloka, and a longer phalashruti section, bringing the total to 55 or even 61. The Vaidika Vignanam, devimahatmya.com and Greenmesg editions present the canonical 47-verse core, while Gita Press and certain Bengali Chandi-paths use the expanded version. The textual content of the protective verses themselves does not differ. (Sources: devimahatmya.com; vignanam.org; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: Which goddess is assigned to which body part in Devi Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The anga-nyasa portion of the Kavacham systematically assigns specific Devi-forms to body regions. Chamunda protects the head, Varahi the third eye, Aindri the ears, Vaishnavi the nose, Kalika the mouth and teeth, Saraswati the tongue, Mahalakshmi the cheeks and chin, Tvarita the throat, Vajrahasta the shoulders, and so on down to the toes. The phalashruti adds directional guardians: Aindri east, Agneyi south-east, Vaishnavi south, etc. This systematic kavacha-nyasa is why the text is treated as a working tantric prayoga rather than only a stotra; the reciter mentally installs each Devi at the specified region as the corresponding line is chanted. (Sources: vignanam.org; mantravidya.com; Hindupedia Devi Kavacham.)

seoFaqs.question: Can women and householders recite Devi Kavacham, or is it restricted?
seoFaqs.answer: Devi Kavacham is universally accessible and not restricted by gender, caste or ashrama. Unlike certain Tantric Mahavidya kavachas which require initiation, the Saptashati Devi Kavacham is part of the open Puranic Devi liturgy and is regularly recited in households by women and men, particularly during the nine days of Sharada Navaratri and Chaitra Navaratri. The traditional recommendation is to maintain physical cleanliness, face east or north, and recite after morning sandhya. Menstruating women are typically advised by family elders to mentally recite or pause for the cycle, though this is a regional acharya tradition rather than a textual injunction within the Kavacham itself. (Sources: artofliving.org; devshoppe.com; mahaperiyavaa.blog.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Devi Kavacham and Durga Kavach as commonly published?
seoFaqs.answer: In strict textual usage the two terms refer to the same composition, the Devyah Kavacham of the Markandeya Purana embedded in the Saptashati. In popular Hindi devotional publishing the title Durga Kavach is often used for accessibility, sometimes accompanied by a Hindi translation alongside the original Sanskrit. There is also a separately circulating short Durga Kavach of about 16 to 20 verses sold in pamphlet form, which is an abridged householder version focusing only on the protective verses without the introductory Markandeya-Brahma dialogue. Devotees seeking the full canonical text should look for the title Devi Kavacham or Saptashati Kavacham. (Sources: utsavapp.in; mantra.tips; devimahatmya.com.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the phalashruti promise for sustained Devi Kavacham recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti makes specific claims: the reciter is freed from all bhayas (fears), is protected from rakshasas, bhutas, pretas, pisachas, dakinis and shakinis, gains victory in battle and legal disputes, removes graha-pida (planetary afflictions), is granted santana (progeny), wealth and long life, and ultimately attains the supreme abode of Devi. It also states that one who recites the Kavacham daily for one year becomes equal in power to the gods, and one who carries a written copy on the body is protected from all weapons. These traditional phala-claims are why the text is recited not only as a daily practice but also as a parihara during illness, legal trouble or sustained misfortune. (Sources: vignanam.org; devshoppe.com; mantravidya.com.)
